question.text: FPIs are NOT allowed to issue Offshore Derivative Instruments (ODIs) referencing which of the following?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per SEBI guidelines, FPIs shall not be allowed to issue ODIs referencing derivatives. Additionally, no FPI shall be allowed to hedge their ODIs with derivative positions on stock exchanges in India. **The Trap:** ODIs referencing equity shares are permitted, so candidates might think all instruments including derivatives are allowed. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) covers derivative instruments and their regulation in more detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The key restriction is ODIs + derivatives = not permitted. ODIs + equity shares = permitted.
